title: Where Larger Models Excel: The Primacy of Constraint-Guided Reasoning
abstract: arXiv:2606.26108v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Larger language models consistently outperform smaller ones on reasoning benchmarks, yet the reasoning differences underlying this gap remain underexplored. Across benchmarks in mathematics, physics, chemistry, and programming, we observe stable performance gaps: averaged over datasets, Qwen3-32B outperforms Qwen3-8B by 6.43%, while GPT-OSS-120B exceeds GPT-OSS-20B by 7.38%. To study the reasoning differences behind these gains, we develop AdvCluster, an automated framework that identifies questions where the larger model shows a stable advantage, extracts fine-grained advantage descriptions from paired reasoning traces produced by larger and smaller models, and organizes them through semantic clustering with quantitative evaluation and selection guided by a reviewer model. Our analysis yields a systematic taxonomy of larger model reasoning advantages, spanning both common advantages that recur across domains and specialized advantages a

Title: Where Larger Models Excel: The Primacy of Constraint-Guided Reasoning
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26108v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Larger language models consistently outperform smaller ones on reasoning benchmarks, yet the reasoning differences underlying this gap remain underexplored. Across benchmarks in mathematics, physics, chemistry, and programming, we observe stable performance gaps: averaged over datasets, Qwen3-32B outperforms Qwen3-8B by 6.43%, while GPT-OSS-120B exceeds GPT-OSS-20B by 7.38%. To study the reasoning differences behind these gains, we develop AdvCluster, an automated framework that identifies questions where the larger model shows a stable advantage, extracts fine-grained advantage descriptions from paired reasoning traces produced by larger and smaller models, and organizes them through semantic clustering with quantitative evaluation and selection guided by a reviewer model. Our analysis yields a systematic taxonomy of larger model reasoning advantages, spanning both common advantages that recur across domains and specialized advantages a
translated_title: 大模型在何处更胜一筹：约束引导推理的首要地位
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26108v1 公告类型：新  摘要：在推理基准测试中，较大的语言模型始终优于较小的模型，但造成这一差距的推理差异仍未得到充分探索。在数学、物理、化学和编程等基准测试中，我们观察到稳定的性能差距：平均来看，Qwen3-32B 比 Qwen3-8B 高出 6.43%，而 GPT-OSS-120B 比 GPT-OSS-20B 高出 7.38%。为了研究这些增益背后的推理差异，我们开发了 AdvCluster，这是一个自动化框架，用于识别大模型具有稳定优势的问题，从大模型和小模型产生的成对推理轨迹中提取细粒度的优势描述，并通过语义聚类进行组织，同时由评审模型指导进行定量评估和选择。我们的分析得出了大模型推理优势的系统性分类，涵盖了跨领域反复出现的共同优势以及特定领域的专门优势。
tldr: 本文通过开发AdvCluster自动化框架，系统性地识别并分类了大模型相较于小模型在推理上的优势，发现其核心在于更擅长遵循和利用问题中的约束条件进行引导推理。
en_tdlr: This paper develops AdvCluster, an automated framework to systematically identify and categorize the reasoning advantages of larger models over smaller ones, revealing that their core advantage lies in a greater ability to follow and leverage constraints for guided reasoning.
